message:
fix(fnp-python): mask_indices runs in-Rust with numpy fallback; clippy allow for masked_interval_compare

Two changes in crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs:

- mask_indices: rewrite from a straight numpy.mask_indices passthrough
  to an in-Rust implementation that builds an n×n all-true boolean
  base via `build_boolean_array`, lifts it to a numpy array with
  `build_numpy_array_from_ufunc`, invokes the user-supplied
  `mask_func(base, k)` (e.g. numpy.triu / numpy.tril), extracts the
  mask with `extract_numeric_array`, runs `where_nonzero` to get the
  row/col index vectors, and finally lifts the two-tuple result via
  `build_numpy_tuple_from_ufuncs`. The `n` parameter is validated as
  non-negative with a PyValueError carrying the numpy-canonical
  "negative dimensions are not allowed" message.

  A numpy.mask_indices closure is retained as a fallback path and
  invoked when the mask_output cannot be extracted as a numeric array
  (e.g. exotic mask_func returning a non-dense result). The fast path
  covers all builtin numpy.triu / numpy.tril / numpy.diag_indices
  style inputs and keeps parity for k offsets.

- masked_interval_compare: annotated with
  `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`. The function legitimately
  threads x / lower / upper / closed_lower / closed_upper / strict /
  fill_value / dtype through the PyO3 boundary, and clippy's
  too_many_arguments fire on the signature without offering a mechanical
  split. The allow is scoped to this one function rather than crate-wide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
